violations.text: Fish was observed held at 46-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the sushi low-boy, with an ambient temperature of 48 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All items requiring refrigeration were moved to other units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/04/2026

Thermometers are lacking in the sushi low-boy refrigeration unit. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026
The low-boy in the sushi area was in poor repair, not maintaining ambient temperature of 41 degrees or colder. New Violation. Correct By: 02/18/2026

Fish was observed thawing in the hand-washing sink in the sushi area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Fish was moved to refrigeration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
The take-out menus and on-line menus do not have a printed consumer advisory. Establishment has posted notices at menu display and point of sale. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Fish was observed thawing on the counter and in the sink without running water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Rish was placed in refrigeration during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
ROP fish was observed thawing with the packaging intact. Fish was still frozen; staff removed from packaging during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Containers of various foods in the walk-in were observed in direct contact with the food below them. Containers were relocated, all foods were to be fully cooked. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the slicer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
Several floor tiles in the front service area were observed in poor repair. PIC stated landlord is scheduled to repair on 1/27/26. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Menus, menu boards, and point of sale are lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the sushi area. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
Establishment does not have a letter certifying parasite destruction. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; can opener was cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
Establishment is using time as a public health control for sushi rice without approval. Application was provided. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Sushi is served raw. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
Restricted insecticide was observed within establishment. PIC informed to remove. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken wings and rice were placed in the walk-in in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Working containers of spices and sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in basement and rear storage room. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PCS presently once per month. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Containers of sauces, juice, and water were observed stored on the floor in multiple locations throughout the facility. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The ice scoop (top of ice machine) and a knife (between prep unit and prep table) were stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Items were brought to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
Single service items were observed stored under a waste pipe in the basement. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-slicer-refrigerator gaskets-top of dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

The floors and junctures in the kitchen have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026
Several floor tiles in the front service area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026
